global_invariants: Phase 4 proves controlled staging/demo readiness and must not silently become production launch.
Deployment/staging readiness is not production launch.
Do not implement Phase 5 Foundry/module installer, Phase 6 modules, platform AI runtime, new business modules, real outbound WhatsApp, production credentials, or production secret access.

Preserve Phase 1/2/3 protections: Access Core boundaries, Gatekeeper fail-closed behavior, trusted tenant/request context, route organization mismatch denial, Lead Desk scope enforcement, Engagement Gateway WhatsApp boundary, audit/outbox evidence, cross-org denial, runtime route limiting, CORS/security headers/env handling, no x-actor-user-id trusted ingress regression, and no schema/registry drift unless explicitly approved.

Decision/control tickets P4-001 through P4-008 must not modify runtime source, Prisma, contracts, generated registry, dependencies, deployment implementation, secrets, or tests unless reclassified and explicitly approved.
Exact-file planning is required before every edit. Broad globs are inspection hints only.
Maximum concrete capability means prove controlled staging/demo behavior wherever safe; it does not authorize production launch or out-of-scope implementation.
DB-level RLS and Prisma tenant transaction architecture are not reopened by default in Phase 4 unless staging/bootstrap evidence proves service-level isolation is insufficient for operational proof.
Do not create permanent ADRs for every operational detail; use ADRs only for true architecture decisions and use process or artifact decision records for operational choices.

Real .env files, local env files, secret-bearing env files, and production credential files are forbidden. .env.example may be updated only as a non-secret template with exact-file planning when required by the active ticket.

use_adrs_for: auth/session provider path if it changes architecture
DB/bootstrap architecture if it changes platform assumptions
route-limiting infrastructure decision if it changes architecture
use_process_or_artifact_records_for: operational procedure choices that do not change architecture

validation_policy.failure_policy: Any validation failure outside active ticket scope is a stop unless bounded repair is explicitly authorized by the active ticket.

redaction_policy: No production data in backup/restore evidence.
No real production env files in artifacts.
No accidental token/session leakage in browser evidence.
runtime_consistency_chains: env -> runtime config -> staging config -> no-secret validation -> smoke tests
fresh DB -> migrations -> bootstrap/setup -> seeded capabilities/modules -> smoke tests
auth provider path -> request context -> frontend session context -> browser tests
route limiting -> proxy/infra decision -> app limiter fallback -> negative tests
backup -> restore drill -> rollback runbook -> closure evidence
frontend route -> browser test -> visual QA evidence

tickets.title: Baseline and Post-Phase-3 source confirmation
tickets.objective: Create a concrete Phase 4 baseline inventory so execution starts from repo truth, not stale assumptions.
scope: Confirm main/source state, current HEAD, and clean worktree before Phase 4 work.
Record Phase 3 merge commit, Phase 3 micro-hardening commit, Roadmap v2 commit, and Post-Phase-3 source refresh manifest/checksum.
Inventory package scripts, CI workflows, public/protected endpoints, runtime env variables, and Phase 4 inputs from the Phase 3 readiness handoff.
evidence_artifacts: git log evidence for Phase 3 and Roadmap v2 commits
tests_required: No runtime tests. This ticket is inspection and artifact generation only.
acceptance_criteria: All required outputs exist.
Baseline records current main HEAD, Phase 3 merge commit, Roadmap v2 commit, source refresh manifest/checksum, scripts, workflows, endpoints, env vars, and Phase 4 handoff inputs.
No repo-tracked runtime/schema/package files changed.
stop_conditions: Required source refresh manifest or Phase 4 handoff is missing.
Repo state is dirty before ticket work.
Endpoint/env inventory cannot be derived from repo files.
production secret or credential access is required
platform AI runtime work is required
new business module work is required

platform AI runtime work is required
new business module work is required

Exit standard includes clean DB bootstraps, staging/demo runs, smoke tests, browser-rendered proof, visual QA evidence, backup/restore proof, rollback proof, runbook, no-secret audit, and closure package.

Decision cannot define a non-production exit standard.

tests_required: Final full validation ladder.
Secret scan.
Token/session leak scan.
Screenshot redaction confirmation.
Backup artifact no-production-data confirmation.
Checksum verification.
Source ZIP exclusion verification.
Redaction review classifies grep/rg findings before ticket closure.
validation_commands: redaction scan with redaction-review.md classification if artifacts may contain sensitive values
validation_expectation: redaction scan with redaction-review.md classification if artifacts may contain sensitive values
acceptance_criteria: All dependencies are complete.
Final package artifacts exist.
Redaction checks pass: secret scan, token/session leak scan, screenshot redaction confirmation, backup artifact no-production-data confirmation.
Phase 5 readiness handoff exists but no Phase 5 ticket pack is created.
Grep/rg scans are inspection evidence; redaction-review.md or equivalent must classify findings.
Real values fail the ticket.
Placeholders are acceptable only if explicitly identified as placeholders.
stop_conditions: Audit package would include secrets, credentials, production data, or unapproved artifacts.
production secret or credential access is required
platform AI runtime work is required
new business module work is required
Any scan output that appears to contain a real secret, token, credential, database URL, private key, production credential, or session value is a stop condition unless proven to be a placeholder.
tickets.commit_message: P4-GATE close Phase 4 operational proof
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert P4-GATE closure docs/package artifacts only; prior ticket commits remain unless separately reverted.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Defines closure package and redaction checks upfront so the final audit is not invented at the end.
tickets.runtime_consistency_chain: ticket evidence -> final validation -> redaction checks -> audit package -> Phase 5 readiness handoff
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Final audit package, completed audit report, validation summary, redaction checks, and source ZIP must exist; a narrative closure note cannot close Phase 4.
requires_human_approval_if: production secret or credential access is required
platform AI runtime work is required
new business module work is required

failure_classification.scope_blocker: Required work is outside this ticket or Phase 4 boundaries.
failure_classification.approval_blocker: Required work needs explicit human approval before proceeding.
failure_classification.validation_blocker: Required validation fails after bounded repair.
failure_classification.evidence_blocker: Required output or artifact cannot be produced without weakening evidence.

commands_to_run: Run secret scan defined by P4-GATE exact-file plan.
Run token/session leak scan defined by P4-GATE exact-file plan.
Generate phase4-source.zip from committed HEAD with exclusions.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS when every prior ticket is complete, final validation passes, package artifacts exist, redaction/no-secret checks pass, and Phase 4 audit report is evidence-backed.
FAIL when any package artifact is missing, validation fails, secrets/tokens/production data are detected, or Phase 5 implementation begins.
tickets.split_if: Split if final package generation, audit completion, and Phase 5 readiness handoff cannot be completed in one bounded closure ticket.
